title: Agent Resilience — Supervisor, Auto-Restart, and Dashboard Health Status
description: hex-agent is a critical runtime component. When it crashes or loses hub connection, it exits silently with no restart. The dashboard has no visibility into agent health. This workplan adds a supervisor in nexus, reconnection logic in hex-agent, and live health status in the dashboard UI.

phases.name: Agent Supervisor in hex-nexus
phases.description: Add a supervisor loop in nexus that monitors the default agent process and restarts it on crash. This is the most critical fix — ensures hex-agent is always running.

gate.command: cargo build -p hex-nexus --release && cargo test -p hex-nexus

tasks.name: Add AgentSupervisor module to hex-nexus
tasks.description: New module hex-nexus/src/orchestration/agent_supervisor.rs. Wraps spawn_default_agent() in a supervision loop: (1) Spawn agent process, (2) Monitor via waitpid/try_wait in a tokio task, (3) On exit: log reason, wait backoff delay (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s), respawn, (4) On SIGTERM/nexus shutdown: kill agent, don't respawn. Track restart_count and last_restart_at. Expose supervisor state via a method for the dashboard.

tasks.name: Replace spawn_default_agent with AgentSupervisor
tasks.description: In hex-nexus/src/lib.rs, replace the current spawn_default_agent() call with AgentSupervisor::start(). The supervisor runs as a background tokio task. On nexus shutdown, call supervisor.stop() to gracefully kill the agent. Remove the manual child.kill() shutdown logic.

tasks.name: Add supervisor status REST endpoint
tasks.description: GET /api/agents/supervisor — returns { running: bool, pid: u32, restart_count: u32, last_restart_at: string, uptime_secs: u64, backoff_secs: u32 }. Wire into routes/mod.rs.

phases.name: hex-agent Reconnection Logic
phases.description: Instead of exiting on hub connection loss, hex-agent should attempt to reconnect with exponential backoff. This handles transient nexus restarts without needing the supervisor to restart the entire agent process.

tasks.name: Add reconnection loop to hub-managed mode
tasks.description: In hex-agent/src/main.rs, wrap the hub message loop in an outer reconnection loop. When hub_client.recv() returns an error: (1) Log 'Hub connection lost, reconnecting...', (2) Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s, (3) Attempt hub_client.connect() again, (4) On success: re-register agent and resume message loop, (5) On persistent failure after N attempts: exit with error code so supervisor can restart fresh. Preserve conversation state across reconnects.

tasks.name: Add reconnect support to HubClientAdapter
tasks.description: The WebSocket hub client adapter needs a reconnect() method that tears down the old connection and establishes a new one. Check hex-agent/src/adapters/ for the hub client. Add is_connected() polling and reconnect() that reuses the same URL/token. Heartbeat task should detect disconnection and pause until reconnected.

tasks.name: Graceful shutdown signal handling
tasks.description: hex-agent should catch SIGTERM/SIGINT and send HubMessage::Deregister before exiting. Currently it just dies. Add a tokio::signal handler that: (1) Sends deregister to hub, (2) Cancels heartbeat task, (3) Exits cleanly with code 0. This lets nexus distinguish between crash (restart) and intentional shutdown (don't restart).

phases.name: Dashboard Agent Health Status
phases.description: The dashboard must show real-time agent health: live/stale/dead badges, restart count, uptime, and reconnection status. Uses existing heartbeat data from SpacetimeDB agent_registry + new supervisor endpoint.

tasks.name: AgentHealthBar component
tasks.description: New SolidJS component hex-nexus/assets/src/components/AgentHealthBar.tsx. Shows: (1) Green/yellow/red dot for live/stale/dead based on last heartbeat, (2) Agent name + uptime, (3) Restart count badge (if > 0, show warning), (4) Status text: idle/thinking/executing. Subscribes to SpacetimeDB agent_heartbeat table for real-time updates.

tasks.name: Supervisor status widget
tasks.description: Widget in the dashboard sidebar or header that shows supervisor status from GET /api/agents/supervisor. Shows: (1) 'Agent: running' or 'Agent: restarting...' with spinner, (2) Restart count with tooltip showing times, (3) PID for debugging. Polls every 10s or uses WebSocket event 'agent_restarted'.

tasks.name: Wire health components into dashboard layout
tasks.description: Add AgentHealthBar to the dashboard header (always visible). Add SupervisorStatus to the sidebar. When agent is dead, show a prominent banner: 'Agent offline — restarting...' with countdown timer based on backoff. Add WebSocket listeners for agent_spawned, agent_connected, agent_disconnected events.

phases.name: Remote Agent Resilience
phases.description: Extend the supervisor and health monitoring to cover remote agents (like bazzite). Remote agents that stop heartbeating should be flagged in the dashboard and optionally re-spawned via SSH.

tasks.name: Remote agent health monitoring
tasks.description: In the heartbeat cleanup task (hex-nexus/src/coordination/cleanup.rs), when a remote agent goes stale/dead: (1) Update its status in SpacetimeDB, (2) Broadcast WebSocket event 'agent_stale' / 'agent_dead', (3) Dashboard shows the agent turning yellow then red. Distinguishable from local agent crashes.

tasks.name: Optional remote agent auto-reconnect
tasks.description: When a known remote agent (previously connected via /api/agents/connect) goes dead, nexus can optionally attempt to re-spawn it via SSH (using the stored host from the connect registration). Add a config flag: auto_reconnect_remote: bool (default: false). When enabled, call spawn_remote_agent for dead remote agents. Store remote agent metadata (host, project_dir) in SpacetimeDB for reconnection.

parallel_opportunities: P1.1 + P2.1 + P2.2 (supervisor and reconnect are independent)

must_have: Dashboard shows agent health in real-time (live/stale/dead)
hex-agent survives transient nexus restarts via reconnection
